channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kristi Noem visiting El Salvador to tour Terrorist Confinement Center
item.description: Noem's slated to meet with El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele, who the administration has touted as a great partner.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Blue state sanctuary laws enabled illegal 'abolish ICE' activist to evade capture, says local DA
item.description: Colorado District Attorney George Brauchler is speaking out against sanctuary policies in his state that he says enabled activist Jeanette Vizguerra to evade capture, “making a mockery of the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 'state secrets privilege' sounds mysterious. Here's what it is and how it works
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is invoking a powerful tool in seeking to cut off a judge's inquiry into whether it defied his order to turn around planes carrying Venezuelan migrants who were being deported from the United States.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NPR and PBS heads answer questions on Capitol Hill regarding allegations of bias
item.description: The CEOs of the largest U.S. public broadcasting networks appeared before a House subcommittee chaired by Republican Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman and David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Top agency staffer trying to block crucial Trump directive once managed DEI team
item.description: Republicans are fuming that the GAO's general counsel who shut down President Trump's request to supersede California's gas emission laws once managed DEI initiatives for the agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Researchers in limbo as Columbia bows to Trump’s demands in bid to restore $400M federal funding
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When President Donald Trump canceled $400 million in funding to Columbia University over its handling of student protests against Israel's war in Gaza, much of the financial pain fell on researchers a train ride away from the school's campus, working on things like curing cancer and
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Mike Johnson and Jim Jordan could hit back at judges blocking Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans are looking at multiple lanes to take on activist judges blocking President Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans in Mike Waltz's old district aren't mad at him or Trump over Yemen Signal chat
item.description: DELAND, Fla. (AP) — As Washington was roiled by the news that one of President Donald Trump’s top aides added a journalist to a group chat discussing military plans, many Republicans going to the polls to replace that White House aide’s old U.S. House seat have brushed off the story.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s new executive order could upend voting
item.description: The order tests the power of Trump’s authority and would require voters using a federal form to show proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. It’s sure to be tested in court.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: Kelly, Gallego call on Hegseth to resign over war plans Signal chat
item.description: Sens. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) and Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) called on Wednesday for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign after The Atlantic revealed texts he sent on the Signal group chat used to lay out plans for attacks against the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HHS could freeze $27.5 million in family-planning grants: Report
item.description: Action from the Robert F. Kennedy Jr.-led agency would affect a network of 4,000 clinics around the country.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump portrait to be removed from Colorado Capitol after president's criticism
item.description: President Trump has criticized a portrait of himself hanging in the Colorado Capitol, suggesting it was intentionally distorted, while Republican lawmakers have asked for it to be removed.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Nassau Democrats block County Executive Blakeman's $400M plan
item.description: LONG ISLAND, N.Y. (PIX11) – Nassau Democrats have accused County Executive Bruce Blakeman of withholding critical first responder funding for their districts – and have refused to approve his $400 million plan for the county.
item.dc:creator: Kirstin Cole, PIX 11

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: 2 months into Trump's second administration, the news industry faces challenges from all directions
item.description: During the first Trump administration, many journalists worried most about being called “fake news.” They now face a more assertive President Donald Trump in his second term as a blitz of action by his new administration has journalists on their heels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's plan to dismantle Education Dept. criticized by Nevada Dems
item.description: Nevada Democrats are criticizing President Donald Trump's effort to dismantle the US Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks DOGE access to Social Security records
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland on Thursday temporarily blocked the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing millions of Americans’ personal data stored by the Social Security Administration (SSA). In a scathing, 137-page opinion, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Florida lawmakers consider legislation that looks to limit DEI policies
item.description: As federal agencies are being directed to terminate all equity-related grants or contracts on the national level, state lawmakers in Tallahassee are considering legislation that centers around limitations on diversity, equity, and inclusion requirements, otherwise known as DEI.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
